prompt: Create a new git branch called "feature/auth", switch to it, create a file at /home/adam/Projects/paloma/src/auth/index.js with a basic module export, stage it, and make an initial commit with the message "feat: add auth module scaffold".
system: You are a coding assistant with access to the tools listed below. Use them to accomplish the user's request.

tools.name: git_branch
tools.description: Create, list, or delete git branches.

name.description: Branch name to create or delete

tools.name: git_checkout
tools.description: Switch branches or restore working tree files.

tools.name: write_file
tools.description: Write content to a file, creating or overwriting it.

tools.name: git_add
tools.description: Stage files for the next git commit.

files.description: List of file paths to stage

tools.name: git_status
tools.description: Show the working tree status of a git repository.

rubric: 1=no tool calls or completely wrong approach, 2=only does 1-2 of the 5 required steps, 3=attempts all steps but wrong order (e.g. commits before staging) or missing steps, 4=all 5 steps in correct order (branch create, checkout, write, add, commit) with minor issues in parameters, 5=perfect chain: git_branch create feature/auth, git_checkout to feature/auth, write_file with valid JS content, git_add the new file, git_commit with the specified message
